title: The Winter Milky Way over the Rockies
explanation: The bright band of the winter Milky Way arches over a snow-covered ridge, with Orion and the Pleiades prominent in the cold, clear sky.

title: Orion Rising above a Frozen Lake
explanation: Orion climbs the eastern sky in this long-exposure image, its belt and the Orion Nebula visible to the naked eye under dark skies.

title: The Pleiades Star Cluster
explanation: The Seven Sisters, an open cluster of hot blue stars wrapped in reflection nebulosity, sits high overhead on winter nights.

title: A Quarter Moon and Earthshine
explanation: A waxing crescent shows earthshine on its dark limb, sunlight reflected from Earth faintly lighting the unlit lunar surface.

title: Geminid Meteors over the Flathead
explanation: Meteors from the Geminid shower streak across a dark December sky above the Flathead Range.

title: Andromeda, the Nearest Spiral Galaxy
explanation: M31, the Andromeda Galaxy, is faintly visible to the unaided eye as a smudge of light 2.5 million light-years away.

title: The Cold Clear Sky of a New Moon
explanation: Around new moon the sky is darkest, revealing thousands of stars and the faint glow of the zodiacal light.

title: Jupiter and Its Galilean Moons
explanation: Jupiter shines brightly in the winter sky; even small binoculars reveal its four largest moons in a line.

explanation: Orion climbs the eastern sky in this long-exposure image, its belt and the Orion Nebula visible to the naked eye under dark skies.

title: The Pleiades Star Cluster
explanation: The Seven Sisters, an open cluster of hot blue stars wrapped in reflection nebulosity, sits high overhead on winter nights.

title: A Quarter Moon and Earthshine
explanation: A waxing crescent shows earthshine on its dark limb, sunlight reflected from Earth faintly lighting the unlit lunar surface.

title: Geminid Meteors over the Flathead
explanation: Meteors from the Geminid shower streak across a dark December sky above the Flathead Range.

title: Andromeda, the Nearest Spiral Galaxy
explanation: M31, the Andromeda Galaxy, is faintly visible to the unaided eye as a smudge of light 2.5 million light-years away.

title: The Cold Clear Sky of a New Moon
explanation: Around new moon the sky is darkest, revealing thousands of stars and the faint glow of the zodiacal light.

title: Jupiter and Its Galilean Moons
explanation: Jupiter shines brightly in the winter sky; even small binoculars reveal its four largest moons in a line.
